howItWorks.intro: This flexibility score calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Rating
rules.formula: Based on deviation from age/gender average norms
rules.explanation: Your sit-and-reach score compared to normative data from fitness testing standards.

howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: How do I do the sit-and-reach test?
faq.answer: Sit on the floor with legs straight and feet flat against a box or wall. Reach forward slowly as far as possible with both hands. Measure the distance past your toes (positive) or before your toes (negative). Take the best of 3 attempts.

faq.question: What is a good sit-and-reach score?
faq.answer: For adult males, 25+ cm is above average and 34+ cm is excellent. For females, 29+ cm is above average and 37+ cm is excellent. Flexibility naturally decreases with age but is highly trainable at any age.

faq.question: Is the Flexibility Score Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard fitness and training formulas trusted by athletes and fitness enthusiasts. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What sit-and-reach distance should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate sit-and-reach distance value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Flexibility Score Calculator?
sections.body: The Flexibility Score Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your sit-and-reach distance, age, gender, you get instant results including reach distance, flexibility rating, approximate percentile. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting reach distance right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your sit-and-reach distance and need to find the right reach distance. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Sit-and-Reach Distance (cm)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your sit-and-reach distance in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Age
howToSteps.description: Type or select your age in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Gender
howToSteps.description: Type or select your gender in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your reach distance, flexibility rating, approximate percentile. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your sit-and-reach distance before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both reach distance and flexibility rating — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your training.
If you're unsure about your gender, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
